characters.name: Mr. Bennet
characters.description: Father of five daughters, characterized as having quick parts, sarcastic humor, reserve, and caprice. Takes ironic delight in his wife's foolishness.

characters.name: Mrs. Bennet
characters.description: Wife of Mr. Bennet and mother of five daughters. A woman of mean understanding, little information, and uncertain temper whose primary concern is getting her daughters married.

physical.appearance: formerly handsome, still considers herself attractive

characters.name: Mr. Bingley
characters.description: A single young man of large fortune from the north of England who has taken Netherfield Park. Worth four or five thousand a year.

characters.name: Elizabeth Bennet
characters.description: One of the Bennet daughters, favored by her father for having more quickness than her sisters.

characters.name: Jane Bennet
characters.description: One of the Bennet daughters, described by Mrs. Bennet as more handsome than Elizabeth.

characters.name: Lydia Bennet
characters.description: One of the Bennet daughters, described by Mrs. Bennet as more good-humored than Elizabeth.

characters.name: Mrs. Long
characters.description: A neighbor who brings news about Mr. Bingley taking Netherfield Park.

characters.name: Mr. Morris
characters.description: The person with whom Mr. Bingley made the agreement to take Netherfield Park.

characters.name: Sir William Lucas
characters.description: A neighbor who, along with his wife, is determined to visit Mr. Bingley.

characters.name: Lady Lucas
characters.description: Wife of Sir William Lucas, determined to visit Mr. Bingley despite generally not visiting newcomers.

timeline.event: Mr. Bingley comes down in a chaise and four to see Netherfield Park
Mr. Bingley agrees with Mr. Morris to take Netherfield Park
Mrs. Long visits Mrs. Bennet and tells her about Mr. Bingley taking Netherfield
Mrs. Bennet informs Mr. Bennet about Mr. Bingley and tries to persuade him to visit

settings.name: Netherfield Park
settings.description: An estate that has been let to Mr. Bingley, located in the neighborhood. Some of his servants are to be in the house by the end of next week.

settings.name: Bennet household
settings.description: The family home where Mr. and Mrs. Bennet have their conversation about the new neighbor.

world_rules: Single men of fortune are expected to marry
Proper social etiquette requires formal visits to new neighbors before they can visit you
Fathers must visit new male neighbors before their wives and daughters can do so
Marriage is the primary business and concern for women with daughters

voice_profile.style_notes: Opens with a famous ironic statement, uses free indirect discourse, formal language typical of early 19th century literature

plot_threads.thread: Will Mr. Bennet visit Mr. Bingley to enable proper social introductions?
Which of the Bennet daughters might attract Mr. Bingley's romantic interest?
How will the arrival of an eligible bachelor affect the neighborhood dynamics?
